dialogos.texto_en: Hmm...
Mmmmhhh...
What's with those serious faces, you two?\nIs something wrong?
Ah, Uesugi-kun.
Nothing's really wrong...
Well, it definitely looks like something's up.\nYour faces are giving it away.
It's really nothing important.
When we were shopping earlier, the store clerk\ntold us we are 'churakagi' twins.
I think it's a word in Okinawan dialect,\nbut I don't understand the meaning,\nand it's bothering me.
Do you know what it means, Uesugi-kun?
No, I'm not that familiar with it either.\nJust some words I've seen\nin signs and advertisements...
Ah, speaking of signs!\nThe name of the aquarium here is\n'Churaumi Aquarium,' right?
Could it be related to THAT 'chura'?
Ah, that's possible.
'Churaumi' should mean 'beautiful sea.'\nSo it could mean 'beautiful', right?
So, in other words, it could mean \n'beautiful person'...perhaps?
Well then...\nIt seems like we were being complimented.
I feel kind of bad that we didn't realize \nwe were being praised.
Well, there's nothing we can do about it.\nThese words are all new to us.
I've learned a few things since coming to Okinawa,\nbut there are still many words I don't understand.
For example, what have you learned?
'Mensoore' means 'welcome.'\n'Niffee debiru' means 'thank you.'
'Yonna yonna' means 'take it easy,' I think.
I see.\nLooks like your shopping and eating spree paid off.
Isn't that what it's all about though?
Well, words used for tourists\ntend to be biased, aren't they?
That's true, huh.\nI hardly did any shopping myself.
Please don't say such sad things...\nI've lost the will to be angry.
You can still try to learn them now.\nIt might be useful in the future.
Well, if you don't understand\nwhat the other person is saying,\nit can be difficult to respond.
Even though we can just look it up\non our phones now, it's still inconvenient.\nCommunication is the foundation of understanding.
If we weren't able to communicate with words,\nwhat would we do?
As sisters, we more or less understand each other,\nso we might be able to figure something out...
Do you really think so?
Huh?
Even though we're dressed the same now%p-2;――
Our personalities are completely different.\nIt's not like when we were kids and were all the same.
That hypothetical scenario doesn't really matter.\nSomething as unrealistic as suddenly not being\nable to communicate isn't even worth considering.
Well, that's true.\nPlease just forget what we were talking about then.
After all, everyone changes\nlittle by little, don't they?
Yet, no matter how much we change in the future,\nI will still be...
Things like appearance and personality\nchange as we live.\nThat's inevitable.
But even after you've changed,\nthere are still things you can discover, right?
Yes, I can relate to that.
Well, I don't think it's very likely\nthat you'll suddenly stop being able\nto communicate in your language, is it?
It's just a hypothetical scenario.\nDon't worry about it.
After all, everyone changes\nlittle by little, don't they?
Yet, no matter how much we change in the future,\nI will still be...
Even if we're not the same,\nisn't understanding each other the most important thing?\nYou're able to imitate Itsuki to this degree.
Well, I suppose.
Understanding and accepting\nare different things...\nbut we're quintuplets, after all.
That makes things such a hassle for me, though.
Instead of saying that,\nwhy don't you try to make an effort\nto understand a little better?
Well... Maybe you're right.
After all, everyone changes\nlittle by little, don't they?
Yet, no matter how much we change in the future,\nI will still be...
(...This feeling,\n it's somewhat reminiscent of Nino.)
Ah...
I see!\nIf we ever can't communicate with words,\nhow about we use gestures instead?
I see, that makes sense.\nThey say body language is universal.
No, no, there are so many differences\nin the level of communication\nbetween verbal and nonverbal communication, right?
No way! We can manage with effort and enthusiasm!
(Hmm...\n This doesn't feel like Nino,\n but more like Yotsuba...)
Alright then,\nlet me try using my gestures\nto communicate with the shop clerk!
Wait, hold on! Wouldn't that be a little unsettling...?!
(She's already gone...\n Oh no, she's not really going to do that, is she?)
